title: 7 Financial-Sector Outcasts Paying Us up to 12.3%
author: Brett Owens
description: The financial sector has underperformed in 2026 due to proposed credit card interest rate caps and AI-driven concerns about lenders. However, regional banks and alternative financial providers offer attractive dividend yields ranging from 5.7% to 12.3%, presenting contrarian investment opportunities for income-focused investors seeking undervalued financial stocks.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Trading at 10x earnings with 5.7% yield; steady business model with low volatility; recent merger completion adds scale and growth potential in commercial lending
7% yield at 10x earnings; mixed 2026 performance with Q4 beat followed by Q1 miss; dividend growth stalled since 2023; profitability improving but execution concerns remain

7.6% yield; company mid-reinvention with new CEO and stripped-down business model; expected return to profitability in 2026 and 30%+ growth in 2027, but dividend frozen for a decade and requires proof of turnaround

7.6% yield; company mid-reinvention with new CEO and stripped-down business model; expected return to profitability in 2026 and 30%+ growth in 2027, but dividend frozen for a decade and requires proof of turnaround

7.9% yield at 7x earnings; consistent annual dividend increases since 2019; strong net interest income growth; physical footprint comparable to national banks; economic sensitivity presents upside potential
9.6% dividend yield; specialist in special dividends; mentioned as high-yield option but limited detail provided in article
11.2% dividend yield; described as 'one of Wall Street's most hated stocks right now'; high yield suggests market skepticism but limited fundamental analysis provided

12.3% headline yield (annualized from recent distributions); 14x 2026 and 12x 2027 earnings; newly public regional bank with 120+ year history; backed by Ford Financial Fund with strong M&A track record; management prioritizing shareholder returns; expected to normalize to ~7% sustainable yield

title: Lazy Investors Think This Mini-Portfolio Yields 6%, but the Real Payout Is 10%
author: Brett Owens
description: Many dividend investors overlook special and supplemental dividends when calculating true yields. A seven-stock portfolio appears to yield 6% based on headline figures, but actually delivers 10% when special dividends are included. The article examines retailers (Dillard's, Buckle), insurers (AMERISAFE, Old Republic International), and BDCs (Capital Southwest, Fidus Investment, Bain Capital Specialty Finance) that use special dividends to boost returns.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite being a struggling mall retailer, DDS has consistently paid large special dividends ($15-$30 per share annually), boosting true yield to 5.9% versus headline 0.2%. Strong track record of sharing profits when available.

Despite being a struggling mall retailer, DDS has consistently paid large special dividends ($15-$30 per share annually), boosting true yield to 5.9% versus headline 0.2%. Strong track record of sharing profits when available.
Fickle fashion retailer with cyclical pressures, but demonstrates commitment to special dividends ($3 per share in 2026), raising true yield to 9.1% from headline 2.9%.
Profits have declined for two consecutive years with anemic job growth outlook. 2025 special dividend was smallest in a decade at 8.4% true yield, signaling reduced capacity for future specials.
Impressive 45-year dividend growth streak with true yield of 9.4% including specials. Management demonstrates willingness to pay large specials when profits allow, though timing is unpredictable.

Strong BDC with well-covered dividend and 11% true yield including specials. Recently converted to monthly distributions. However, shares trade at 40% premium to NAV, making valuation a significant concern.
Solid BDC with 11.8% true yield including quarterly specials. Outperformed sector by 15 percentage points despite tech exposure. Trades at modest 5% discount to NAV, offering reasonable valuation.

While offering 14.6% true yield with specials, there are warning signs: no specials announced after Q1 2026, earnings projected to barely cover dividend in 2026 and fall short in 2027. Sustainability of high yield is questionable.

title: OLD REPUBLIC DECLARES SECOND QUARTER REGULAR DIVIDEND OF 31.5 CENTS PER SHARE
author: Prnewswire
description: Old Republic International Corporation announced a regular quarterly dividend of 31.5 cents per share, payable June 15, 2026. The company projects a full-year dividend of $1.26 per share in 2026, representing an 8.6% increase from $1.16 per share in 2025. This marks the 45th consecutive year of dividend increases and the 85th year of uninterrupted dividend payments.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company announced an 8.6% year-over-year dividend increase and demonstrated strong commitment to shareholders with 45 consecutive years of dividend increases and 85 years of uninterrupted payments. This reflects financial stability and consistent profitability.

title: 3 Defensive Dividend Stocks to Weather Market Uncertainty
author: Jesse Cohen
description: The article recommends three defensive dividend stocks for navigating market volatility: General Mills (GIS) with a 6.83% yield and 13.8% fair value upside, Clorox (CLX) offering 5.4% yield with 20.8% analyst upside, and Old Republic International (ORI) providing 9.5% yield with strong financial health. These companies feature resilient business models, stable cash flows, and consistent dividend payouts suitable for income-focused investors seeking shelter during economic uncertainty.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite 23% YTD decline, the stock offers attractive 6.83% dividend yield, 13.8% fair value upside, strong ROE of 24.6%, and reliable 50+ year dividend history. Positioned as defensive anchor with counter-cyclical traits and robust brands.

Modest 9% YTD decline demonstrates resilience, 5.4% dividend yield, and significant 20.8% analyst target upside to $107.41. Strong portfolio of essential consumer products and innovation pipeline support defensive positioning.

Highest dividend yield at 9.5% with history of special dividends, strong financial health score of 2.85 ('GOOD'), diversified insurance operations, and prudent risk management. Well-positioned for volatile environments with capital stability.

title: The Best Dividend Stock to Buy With $50 Right Now
author: Adé Hennis
description: Old Republic International (ORI), an insurance company trading under $50, is highlighted as a strong dividend stock. The company increased its annual dividend for the 44th consecutive year and declared a record special dividend of $2.50 per share. Despite a decline in net income in Q3 2025, operating income rose, demonstrating underlying strength in insurance operations. The stock has gained 90% over three years and 25% year-to-date, with analyst Piper Sandler raising its price target to $51.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The company demonstrates consistent dividend growth (44 consecutive years of increases), record special dividend payouts, strong stock performance (90% gain over 3 years, 25% YTD), rising operating income, and analyst upgrades with price target increases to $51, indicating confidence in future growth potential.
